title: Quantifiers: much, many, little, few
explanation:
Quantifiers

  • many + countable plural: many books, many people
  • much + uncountable: much time, much money
  • a lot of / lots of — both: a lot of friends, a lot of water
  • few / a few + countable: few = almost none; a few = some
  • little / a little + uncountable: little = almost none; a little = some

questions.text: She has [*a lot of|much|little] friends at school.
questions.feedback: Friends = countable; a lot of works.

questions.text: There are [*a few|a little|much] apples in the fridge.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: How ____ people came to the party? | *many | much | little
There is ____ sugar in my coffee, thanks. | *a little | a few | many
Very ____ students failed the exam. | *few | little | much
Did you buy ____ bread? | *any | many | few
I have [*too many|too much|too few] things to do.
She spoke very [*little|few|many] during the meeting.
[*How much|How many|How few] does the ticket cost?
We saw [*a lot of|much|little] interesting places.
